raw_output_payload.reasoning: We need a bash command to count files named job.history under /testbed that contain 'FAIL'. Use find and grep -l and wc -l. Command: find /testbed -type f -name 'job.history' -exec grep -q 'FAIL' {} \; -print | wc -l. But need to count only those that match. Use -exec grep -l 'FAIL' {} + then wc -l. So: find /testbed -type f -name job.history -exec grep -l 'FAIL' {} + | wc -l. Output one line.
raw_output_payload.reasoning_content: We need a bash command to count files named job.history under /testbed that contain 'FAIL'. Use find and grep -l and wc -l. Command: find /testbed -type f -name 'job.history' -exec grep -q 'FAIL' {} \; -print | wc -l. But need to count only those that match. Use -exec grep -l 'FAIL' {} + then wc -l. So: find /testbed -type f -name job.history -exec grep -l 'FAIL' {} + | wc -l. Output one line.

task.query: Count the number of files named 'job.history' under '/testbed' directory tree that match 'FAIL' in their contents
task.gold: find /testbed -name job.history | xargs grep -l FAIL | wc -l
